琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Located within [[:en:Kotohiki Park|Kotohiki Park]], itself part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], there is an [[:en:Etiology|aetiological legend]] that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|koto]] on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''[[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-no-kuni]] Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher=[[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji City]] |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] 2]] 2]] 2]][[Category|Kotohiki-hachiman-gū}} * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] * [[Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:703]] [[Category:8th-century religious buildings and structures]] [[Category:New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:琴弾八幡宮]] [[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Located within [[:en:Kotohiki Park|Kotohiki Park]], itself part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], there is an [[:en:Etiology|aetiological legend]] that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|koto]] on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''[[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-no-kuni]] Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher=[[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji City]] |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons 琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Located within [[:en:Kotohiki Park|Kotohiki Park]], itself part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], there is an [[:en:Etiology|aetiological legend]] that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|koto]] on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''[[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-no-kuni]] Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher=[[:en:Kan'onji, Kagawa|Kan'onji City]] |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons 琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Located within {{ill|Kotohiki Park|en|Kotohiki Park|WD=Q3363843|lt=Kotohiki Park}}, itself part of the {{ill|Setonaikai National Park|en|Setonaikai National Park|WD=Q1140583|lt=Setonaikai National Park}}, there is an {{ill|Etiology|en|Etiology|WD=Q156318|lt=aetiological legend}} that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=koto}} on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-no-kuni}} Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher={{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji City}} |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] 2]] 2]] 2]][[Category|Kotohiki-hachiman-gū}} * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} * [[Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]] {{Commons category|Kotohiki-hachiman-gū}} [[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:琴弾八幡宮]] [[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q3199184}} [[Category:703]] {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Located within {{ill|Kotohiki Park|en|Kotohiki Park|WD=Q3363843|lt=Kotohiki Park}}, itself part of the {{ill|Setonaikai National Park|en|Setonaikai National Park|WD=Q1140583|lt=Setonaikai National Park}}, there is an {{ill|Etiology|en|Etiology|WD=Q156318|lt=aetiological legend}} that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=koto}} on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-no-kuni}} Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher={{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji City}} |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== [[Category:2]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[co:commons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[co:commons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[sn:commons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotohiki Hachimangū
琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Located within {{ill|Kotohiki Park|en|Kotohiki Park|WD=Q3363843|lt=Kotohiki Park}}, itself part of the {{ill|Setonaikai National Park|en|Setonaikai National Park|WD=Q1140583|lt=Setonaikai National Park}}, there is an {{ill|Etiology|en|Etiology|WD=Q156318|lt=aetiological legend}} that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=koto}} on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-no-kuni}} Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher={{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji City}} |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]][[Category|Kotohiki-hachiman-gū}} * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} * [[Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] [[Category:2]] {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Located within {{ill|Kotohiki Park|en|Kotohiki Park|WD=Q3363843|lt=Kotohiki Park|1=Kotohiki Park}}, itself part of the {{ill|Setonaikai National Park|en|Setonaikai National Park|WD=Q1140583|lt=Setonaikai National Park|1=Setonaikai National Park}}, there is an {{ill|Etiology|en|Etiology|WD=Q156318|lt=aetiological legend|1=Etiology}} that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=koto|1=Koto (instrument)}} on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-no-kuni|1=Sanuki Province}} Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher={{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|WD=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also== [[Category:2]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[co:commons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[co:commons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] [[arz:كوتوهيكى هاشتشمنجى]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kotohiki-hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotohiki Hachimangū]][[fr:Kotohiki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:琴弾八幡宮]][[zh:琴彈八幡宮]] {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotohiki Hachimangū
琴弾八幡宮 | image = Kotohiki-hachiman entrance.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] of Kotohiki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.131613|133.647799|format=dms|display=title,inline|type:railwaystation_region:JP}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] shrine | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = 703 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1 Yahata-chō 1-chōme, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kotohiki Hachimangū'''|琴弾八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Located within {{ill|Kotohiki Park|en|Kotohiki Park|qid=Q3363843|lt=Kotohiki Park|1=Kotohiki Park}}, itself part of the {{ill|Setonaikai National Park|en|Setonaikai National Park|qid=Q1140583|lt=Setonaikai National Park|1=Setonaikai National Park}}, there is an {{ill|Etiology|en|Etiology|qid=Q156318|lt=aetiological legend|1=Etiology}} that sees the god [[Hachiman]] appearing to the eighth-century monk Nisshō Shōnin while he was playing a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|qid=Q289037|lt=koto|1=Koto (instrument)}} on board ship. The legend is depicted in the ''{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-no-kuni|1=Sanuki Province}} Shippōzan Hachiman Kotobikigū engi'', records that are an [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Property of the city.]]{{cite book |title=The 88 Temples of Shikoku Island, Japan |pages=123f |author=Miyata, Taisen |publisher=Koyasan Buddhist Temple, Los Angeles |year=2006}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Kan'onji City |publisher={{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} |accessdate=8 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150220203305/http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html |archivedate=20 February 2015 }} As a result of the [[Shinbutsu bunri|enforced separation of Buddhism and Shinto]] during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the enshrined image of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] was transferred to nearby [[Kannon-ji]].{{cite book |title=Shikoku henro |author=Hirahata Yoshio |publisher=Manganji Kyōkabu |page=218 |year=2010 |language=Japanese }} There is a lively [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.chousa.net/ |title=Kotohiki Hachimangū Daisai / Kannon-ji Chōsa Matsuri |publisher=Chōsa Matsuri |accessdate=8 February 2012}} ==See also==
